title: BigBear.ai Stock Down 26% YTD – Should You Invest in BBAI Stock in 2026? Can the Ask Sage Deal Lead to a Rebound?
text: BigBear.ai (BBAI) has declined approximately 26% year-to-date, despite completing its strategic Ask Sage acquisition in January 2026. The Ask Sage platform brings approximately $25 million recurring revenue annually to BigBear, marking a pivotal transition from project-based government contracting to a more predictable SaaS model. This acquisition represents management's strategic pivot toward scalable software economics. Gross margins expanded to roughly 34% in Q1, primarily driven by Ask Sage's higher-margin product offerings. However, the company still faces operating losses as administrative expenses exceed gross profit. First-quarter results proved underwhelming with flat revenue at $34.4 million. The underlying dynamics revealed weakness in U.S. Army programs that overshadowed Ask Sage growth, underscoring BigBear's vulnerability to lumpy defense spending cycles. The backlog did increase sequentially but remains depressed compared to prior-year levels. BigBear maintains substantial financial flexibility with over $430 million in cash and marketable securities, coupled with essentially zero long-term debt. This cushion provides management runway to execute their platform strategy without immediate dilution pressure. Investors should monitor: recurring revenue scaling beyond current Ask Sage run rates, gross margin trajectory toward 50%+ levels, sustained year-over-year backlog growth, accelerated Department of Defense award cycles under new procurement timelines, and path to sustainable operating profitability.
